request_payload.requirement_summary: Show the total count of currently visible service requests above the list in the Service Request Tracker frontend. This count must update dynamically when the request list changes (e.g., after adding or removing requests). The change is purely a frontend enhancement — no backend, database, or API layer modifications are required.

functional_requirements.title: Display visible request count
functional_requirements.description: The system SHALL display the total number of currently visible service requests above the request list.

functional_requirements.title: Dynamic count update
functional_requirements.description: The displayed count SHALL update automatically when the list of visible service requests changes.

functional_requirements.title: Zero-state handling
functional_requirements.description: When no service requests are visible, the system SHALL display a count of '0' above the list.

functional_requirements.title: Count placement
functional_requirements.description: The count SHALL be displayed above the request list and visually associated with the list.

functional_requirements.title: Accessible count display
functional_requirements.description: The count element SHALL be accessible via screen readers using appropriate ARIA attributes or semantic HTML.

non_functional_requirements.title: Performance — no re-render overhead
non_functional_requirements.description: The count display SHALL NOT introduce noticeable re-render overhead and SHALL use existing list data.

non_functional_requirements.title: Maintainability — minimal code change
non_functional_requirements.description: Implementation SHALL be contained within existing frontend files.

non_functional_requirements.title: Consistency — visual design
non_functional_requirements.description: The count display SHALL follow the existing application design system.

non_functional_requirements.title: Reliability — count accuracy
non_functional_requirements.description: The count SHALL always reflect the exact number of items rendered in the visible list.

assumptions: List data available as in-memory array
open_questions: Label format? ('Showing N requests' vs just number)

epic_candidate.title: Show Request Count
epic_candidate.description: Add a visible count of service requests above the list so platform engineers can quickly assess workload volume at a glance.
epic_candidate.label: epic

acceptance_criteria.description: A count displaying the number of visible service requests is rendered above the request list.
The displayed count updates automatically when a new service request is added and appears in the list.
The displayed count updates automatically when a service request is removed from the visible list.
When the list contains no visible requests, the count displays '0' (or '0 requests').
The count is positioned above the request list and is visually associated with it (e.g., aligned with the list container, using consistent spacing).
The count element is announced by screen readers when the page loads and when the count changes.
No additional API calls or network requests are made to compute or update the count.
The count always matches the number of list items rendered in the DOM (no off-by-one or stale values).

given_when_then.scenario: View request count on page load
given_when_then.given: The Service Request Tracker page is loaded with 5 service requests visible in the list
given_when_then.then: a count of '5' (or '5 requests') is displayed above the list

given_when_then.scenario: Count updates after adding a request
given_when_then.given: the request list currently shows 3 visible requests and a count of '3'
given_when_then.when: a new service request is created and appears in the list, making it 4 visible items

given_when_then.given: the request list currently shows 5 visible requests and a count of '5'
given_when_then.when: a request is removed and the visible list now has 4 items

given_when_then.given: the Service Request Tracker page is loaded with no service requests in the list
given_when_then.then: a count of '0' (or '0 requests') is displayed above the empty list

given_when_then.scenario: Screen reader announces count on load
given_when_then.given: a user accesses the page using a screen reader
given_when_then.when: the page loads and the request list renders
given_when_then.then: the screen reader announces the count (e.g., '5 requests' or 'Showing 5 requests')

given_when_then.given: a screen reader user is on the page with a visible count of '3'
given_when_then.when: a new request is added and the count changes to '4'
given_when_then.then: the screen reader announces the updated count

verification_checks.description: Verify that the count element exists in the DOM and is positioned above the request list container.
Verify that the count matches the length of the rendered list array/data source.
Verify that adding an item to the list increments the count by exactly 1.
Verify that removing an item from the list decrements the count by exactly 1.
Verify that no network requests are triggered when the count updates.
Verify that the count element has appropriate ARIA attributes (e.g., aria-live='polite' or role='status').
Verify that the count reads '0' or '0 requests' when the list is empty.

edge_cases.description: Empty list: count shows '0' — no crash, no blank, no error message.
Single item: count shows '1' with grammatically correct singular label (e.g., '1 request' not '1 requests').
Very large list (e.g., 10,000+ items): count displays correctly without performance degradation.
Rapid mutations (add/remove in quick succession): count remains accurate after all mutations settle.
List filtered to zero: if list becomes empty due to client-side filtering, count shows '0' (though filtering is out of scope for this feature, the count should handle data changes gracefully).

unit_testing: Test that the count component renders with the correct number prop
Test that the count displays '0' when the list is empty
Test that the count element has the correct ARIA attributes
integration_testing: Test that adding a request updates the count
Test that removing a request updates the count
Test that no extra network calls are made during count updates
Test component re-render behavior with count changes
manual_testing: Visual verification of count position and styling
Screen reader verification (VoiceOver, NVDA, or JAWS)
Verify count on page load with varying list sizes
Verify count updates after creating a new request via the UI

definition_of_done: All acceptance criteria (AC-001 through AC-008) pass
All GWT scenarios (GWT-001 through GWT-006) pass
Verification checks (VC-001 through VC-007) are executed and passing
Edge cases (EC-001 through EC-005) are reviewed and handled
Unit and integration tests are written and passing

refined_epic_candidate.title: [Epic] Show Request Count
refined_epic_candidate.description: Add a visible count of service requests above the list so platform engineers can quickly assess workload volume at a glance. This is a frontend-only enhancement that derives the count from the existing list data.
refined_epic_candidate.label: epic

user_stories.title: [User Story] Show visible request count
user_stories.description: As a platform engineer, I want to see the total number of visible service requests so that I can quickly understand the current workload.
user_stories.label: user-story

task_candidates.title: [Task] Add JSX count display
task_candidates.description: Add a count display element (e.g., a paragraph or span showing the number of visible requests) above the ServiceRequestList component. Derive the count from the existing list data source (array length).

task_candidates.title: [Task] Add CSS styling for count element
task_candidates.description: Add styling for the count display consistent with the existing design system. Ensure proper spacing, typography, and positioning above the list.

task_candidates.title: [Task] Add accessibility support for count
task_candidates.description: Add ARIA attributes (aria-live='polite', role='status') or use semantic HTML so screen readers announce the count and its updates. Ensure the count is accessible on load and on change.

task_candidates.title: [Task] Add unit and integration tests for count
task_candidates.description: Write unit tests for count rendering, zero-state, pluralization, and ARIA attributes. Write integration tests for count updates on add/remove and verifying no extra network calls.

human_readable:
=== STAGE 2: ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA GENERATION ===

Business Intent: Show Request Count

Acceptance Criteria:
- AC-001: Count displayed above request list
- AC-002: Count updates when request added
- AC-003: Count updates when request removed
- AC-004: Zero state shows '0'
- AC-005: Visual association with list
- AC-006: Screen reader accessible
- AC-007: No extra API calls
- AC-008: Count always matches DOM

Given/When/Then Scenarios:
- GWT-001: View count on page load
- GWT-002: Count updates after adding request
- GWT-003: Count updates after request removal
- GWT-004: Zero state display
- GWT-005: Screen reader announces count on load
- GWT-006: Screen reader announces count change

Task Candidates:
1. [Task] Add JSX count display
2. [Task] Add CSS styling for count element
3. [Task] Add accessibility support for count
4. [Task] Add unit and integration tests for count

User Story: As a platform engineer, I want to see the total number of visible service requests so that I can quickly understand the current workload.

Next Stage: Solution Architecture (Stage 3)
